bugs_found: phi regalloc clash (3+ phis share register)

previous_rounds.summary: Task 3 tier-up policy flip caused fib/ackermann/mutual_recursion to hang at Tier 2; reverted.
Diagnosed tier2 hang: BuildGraph silently drops CALL B=0 args. Landed Unpromotable gate.
Phase 1 diagnostic: profiled all 5 float benchmarks.
Phase 2: FPR-resident infra. Mandelbrot -2.62%, aggregate -1.88%, zero regressions.
Phase 4: LICM pass landed. Wall-time unchanged: mandelbrot -1.6%.
LICM-invariant FPR carry. Mandelbrot -6.2%, nbody -12.2%, spectral -15.2%, matmul -12.7%.
GPR-resident int counter + fused compare+branch. fibonacci_iterative -7.4%, matmul -2.7%, math_intensive -3.5%. Float loops ~1-2% (superscalar hides insn-level savings).
Phase 3+5: B=0 fix kept (correctness). Tier 2 net-negative for recursive functions — 27-50% regressions, reverted.
Phase 3: Guard insertion code correct at IR level, but feedback never collected — Tier 1 doesn't collect feedback, interpreter never runs with BaselineCompileThreshold=1.
Native ArrayBool/ArrayFloat fast paths in emitGetTableNative/emitSetTableNative. Sieve -18-25% (0.227s→0.186s). Eliminated exit-resume for bool/float typed arrays.
Tier 1 float/bool GETTABLE/SETTABLE fast paths + feedback infra + Tier 2 raw-int key/const-value bypasses. matmul -80.2%, spectral -54.0%, sieve -55.9%.
Re-enabled OSR with LoopDepth >= 2 gate. mandelbrot -80%, spectral_norm -64%, matmul -29%, fannkuch -16%. mandelbrot now 1.27x from LuaJIT.
Load Elimination (block-local GetField CSE) + TypeFloat guard fix. nbody -26%, broad 17-49% across GetField-heavy benchmarks.
GETFIELD feedback recording in Go exit handlers + shape guard dedup. nbody -8.3%, table_field_access -23.5%, spectral_norm -8.7%.

LICM GetField hoisting + store-to-load forwarding. Infra landed correctly but nbody target unchanged: inner loop has SetField on same objects, blocking hoisting. S2L forwarding subsumed by existing CSE.

Table validation dedup (tableVerified) + array kind feedback + kind-specialized emit. Infra landed correctly. Sieve unchanged: branch predictor already handled dispatch cascade for free. Secondary: fannkuch -10%, table_array_access -6%.

Native GetGlobal dispatch + LICM hoisting + Tier 1 self-call optimization. nbody -49% (0.555s→0.284s), fib -90% (1.365s→0.135s), fib_recursive -90%. ackermann +137% regression from self-call overhead on tight recursion.
Production typing diagnostic confirms Scenario A (feedback works). R(0) pin to X22 + NaN-boxed closure cache in X21. nbody -8.1%, broad 8-23% across 18/22 benchmarks from slot-0 register pinning.
Float param guards + GuardType CSE + LICM whitelist. nbody -10.3%, spectral -8.7%, table_field_access -17.3%, broad improvements. Bugfix: mixed int/float params excluded from float speculation.

Guard hoisting in LICM + cross-block shape/table verification propagation (single-predecessor). Infra landed correctly. A/B testing confirmed zero wall-time change — M4 superscalar hides removed guards (predicted branches, low IPC cost). Also fixed OpAppend/OpSetList LICM alias, tableVerified exit-resume clearing.

Forward KnownInt analysis + int-specialized ADD/SUB/MUL/EQ/LT/LE templates. ackermann -5.2% (0.595s→0.564s), fib -4.3% (0.140s→0.134s). mutual_recursion +4.9% regression (guard overhead exceeds body saving on small protos). Float benchmarks untouched.

Harness repair: median-of-N benchmark runner + re-baseline with median-of-5. Confirmed R24 wins (ackermann -6.2%, fib -5.0%). Phantom regressions were single-shot noise. Stretch: int-spec overflow deopt now resumes at guard PC (not pc=0) via vm.ResumeFromPC — prevents side-effect replay.

Go goroutines start at 8KB stack; JIT cannot call morestack; NativeCallDepth=48 is goroutine-stack budget, not paranoia. SP-floor approach fails (7KB residual << 2MB floor). Task 0 fixture committed at 878e64a (923 insns baseline). Tasks 1-3 aborted. See opt/premise_error.md.

ctx.Constants STR moved from shared restore_done join to normal-call-only block. ackermann −5.2%, fib −3.8%, mutual_recursion −4.2% (vs single-shot baseline; true self-call signal ~0.5-1.3%). Item 1a of tier1-call-overhead closed. No regressions.

ctx.Regs STR elided at self-call setup (tier1_call.go:389), lazy-flushed in emitBaselineOpExitCommon. Static +3 insns (6 op-exit sites, not 3 as predicted). Wall-time vs true predecessor 39b5ef3: ackermann 0.272→0.273, fib 1.421→1.446 — within noise. VERIFY incorrectly compared against stale a388f78 baseline; user-led bisect found 598bc1e (deep-recursion correctness fix) as true pivot: ackermann -50% / fib +988% on same self-call path. Re-baselined to 5b5336c.

R29 diagnostic round for fib +988% regression. Root cause confirmed: handleNativeCallExit fires exactly once per benchmark (cold GETGLOBAL miss during first self-call), permanently zeros DirectEntryPtr, and the 598bc1e CBZ guard at tier1_call.go:316-317 then routes every subsequent self-call through emitBaselineOpExitCommon(OP_CALL) — ~29M Go/JIT roundtrips for fib(35). Ack survives because call depth is tiny. Deliverables: opt/knowledge/r29-fib-root-cause.md + tier1_fib_dump_test.go (fibTotalInsnBaseline=635, 76 lines). No production code touched. R30 picks between (A) drop self-call CBZ or (B) HasOpExits proto flag. Ceiling decay: recursive_call reset (3 rounds absent). Results: coroutine_bench -16.2% (high-variance, ignored); all other deltas ±3% noise.

R30 Task 1 landed isTransientOpExit(OP_GETGLOBAL) predicate gating DirectEntryPtr clearing + globalCacheGen bump in handleNativeCallExit. Curated correctness gate passed during IMPLEMENT, but full ./internal/methodjit/... run in VERIFY caught TestTier2RecursionDeeperFib crashing with 'fatal error: unknown caller pc' — unbounded re-entry into handleNativeCallExit under Tier 2/Tier 1 cross-tier recursion corrupts the Go stack. Reverted at commit 4455fcf. Lessons: (1) every IMPLEMENT task must run the full package, not a curated subset; (2) R29's 'GETGLOBAL fires once per benchmark' invariant was Tier 1-only and silently broke under CompileTier2. fib still 1.437s (10x pre-598bc1e pivot). Post-revert benchmarks within noise of R29 baseline.

R31 SimplifyPhisPass (Braun Algorithm 5 — Tarjan SCC over phi-subgraph, cardinality-1 collapse) landed in commit c375913. 226 LOC impl + 450 LOC tests; wired at two sites in both RunTier2Pipeline and NewTier2Pipeline. All tests green, evaluator PASS. Sieve 0.085→0.084 (-1.2%, below 5% floor). Root cause of miss: the diagnostic at opt/diagnostics/r31-sieve.md was produced by the known-stale profileTier2Func pipeline (constraints.md flag); production compileTier2 already collapses these phis via tryRemoveTrivialPhi + ConstProp/DCE, so the new pass catches nothing on sieve. Generic infra kept as safety net for future construction bugs or inliner-introduced SCCs. All other benchmarks within ±3% noise; coroutine_bench +12.2% is known high-variance.

R32 LoopScalarPromotionPass (264 LOC + 296 LOC tests) wired after LICM in both RunTier2Pipeline and NewTier2Pipeline at commit 56b19e7. All tests green, evaluator PASS. nbody 0.248→0.248 (0.0%, target was -4%); no regressions ≥5%. Post-round re-run of TestR32_NbodyLoopCarried on post-pipeline IR shows all 9 loop-carried pairs still present. Root cause of miss: the pass's float-type gate (pass_scalar_promote.go:99 — `if instr.Type == TypeFloat`) rejects every pair because production IR emits `GetField : any` followed by separate `GuardType float`; unit tests hand-constructed TypeFloat directly so they passed while the pass was silently a no-op on real Tier 2 compilation. R33 plan-starter: change the gate to inspect GetField consumers for GuardType float (one-line fix). Cross-round pattern with R31: every new Tier 2 pass needs a real-pipeline diagnostic test, not just synthetic IR.
